properties.description: The timestamp that indicates when the custom attribute definition was created, in RFC 3339 format.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
Seller-oriented description of the custom attribute definition, including any constraints
that the seller should observe. May be displayed as a tooltip in Square UIs. This field is
required if the `visibility` field is `VISIBILITY_READ_ONLY` or `VISIBILITY_READ_WRITE_VALUES`.

properties.name: key
properties.description:
The identifier
of the custom attribute definition and its corresponding custom attributes. This value
can be a simple key, which is the key that is provided when the custom attribute definition
is created, or a qualified key, if the requesting
application is not the definition owner. The qualified key consists of the application ID
of the custom attribute definition owner
followed by the simple key that was provided when the definition was created. It has the
format application_id:simple key.

The value for a simple key can contain up to 60 alphanumeric characters, periods (.),
underscores (_), and hyphens (-).

This field can not be changed
after the custom attribute definition is created. This field is required when creating
a definition and must be unique per application, seller, and resource type.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The name of the custom attribute definition for API and seller-facing UI purposes. The name must
be unique within the seller and application pair. This field is required if the
`visibility` field is `VISIBILITY_READ_ONLY` or `VISIBILITY_READ_WRITE_VALUES`.

properties.name: schema
properties.description:
The JSON schema for the custom attribute definition, which determines the data type of the corresponding custom attributes. For more information,
see [Custom Attributes Overview](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/devtools/customattributes/overview). This field is required when creating a definition.

properties.name: updated_at
properties.description:
The timestamp that indicates when the custom attribute definition was created or most recently updated,
in RFC 3339 format.

properties.name: version
properties.description:
Read only. The current version of the custom attribute definition.
The value is incremented each time the custom attribute definition is updated.
When updating a custom attribute definition, you can provide this field
and specify the current version of the custom attribute definition to enable
[optimistic concurrency](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/common-api-patterns/optimistic-concurrency).

On writes, this field must be set to the latest version. Stale writes are rejected.

This field can also be used to enforce strong consistency for reads. For more information about strong consistency for reads,
see [Custom Attributes Overview](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/devtools/customattributes/overview).

properties.name: visibility
properties.description:
Specifies how the custom attribute definition and its values should be shared with
the seller and other applications. If no value is specified, the value defaults to `VISIBILITY_HIDDEN`.
See [Visibility](#type-visibility) for possible values

properties.name: idempotency_key
properties.description:
A value you specify that uniquely identifies this
order among orders you have created.

If you are unsure whether a particular order was created successfully,
you can try it again with the same idempotency key without
worrying about creating duplicate orders.

For more information, see [Idempotency](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/common-api-patterns/idempotency).

properties.name: order
properties.description:
The order to create. If this field is set, the only other top-level field that can be
set is the `idempotency_key`.

properties.name: closed_at
properties.description: The timestamp for when the order reached a terminal [state](entity:OrderState), in RFC 3339 format (for example "2016-09-04T23:59:33.123Z").

properties.name: created_at
properties.description: The timestamp for when the order was created, at server side, in RFC 3339 format (for example, "2016-09-04T23:59:33.123Z").

properties.name: customer_id
properties.description:
The ID of the [customer](entity:Customer) associated with the order.

You should specify a `customer_id` on the order (or the payment) to ensure that transactions
are reliably linked to customers. Omitting this field might result in the creation of new
[instant profiles](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/customers-api/what-it-does#instant-profiles).

properties.name: discounts
properties.description:
The list of all discounts associated with the order.

Discounts can be scoped to either `ORDER` or `LINE_ITEM`. For discounts scoped to `LINE_ITEM`,
an `OrderLineItemAppliedDiscount` must be added to each line item that the discount applies to.
For discounts with `ORDER` scope, the server generates an `OrderLineItemAppliedDiscount`
for every line item.

__IMPORTANT__: If `LINE_ITEM` scope is set on any discounts in this field, using the deprecated
`line_items.discounts` field results in an error. Use `line_items.applied_discounts`
instead.

properties.name: fulfillments
properties.description:
Details about order fulfillment.

Orders can only be created with at most one fulfillment. However, orders returned
by the API might contain multiple fulfillments.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The order's unique ID.

properties.name: line_items
properties.description: The line items included in the order.

properties.name: location_id
properties.description: The ID of the seller location that this order is associated with.

properties.name: metadata
properties.description:
Application-defined data attached to this order. Metadata fields are intended
to store descriptive references or associations with an entity in another system or store brief
information about the object. Square does not process this field; it only stores and returns it
in relevant API calls. Do not use metadata to store any sensitive information (such as personally
identifiable information or card details).

Keys written by applications must be 60 characters or less and must be in the character set
`[a-zA-Z0-9_-]`. Entries can also include metadata generated by Square. These keys are prefixed
with a namespace, separated from the key with a ':' character.

Values have a maximum length of 255 characters.

An application can have up to 10 entries per metadata field.

Entries written by applications are private and can only be read or modified by the same
application.

For more information, see  [Metadata](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/metadata).

properties.name: net_amount_due_money
properties.description: The net amount of money due on the order.

properties.name: net_amounts
properties.description: The net money amounts (sale money - return money).

properties.name: pricing_options
properties.description:
Pricing options for an order. The options affect how the order's price is calculated.
They can be used, for example, to apply automatic price adjustments that are based on
preconfigured [pricing rules](entity:CatalogPricingRule).

properties.name: reference_id
properties.description:
A client-specified ID to associate an entity in another system
with this order.

properties.name: refunds
properties.description: The refunds that are part of this order.

properties.name: return_amounts
properties.description: The rollup of the returned money amounts.

properties.name: returns
properties.description:
A collection of items from sale orders being returned in this one. Normally part of an
itemized return or exchange. There is exactly one `Return` object per sale `Order` being
referenced.

properties.name: rewards
properties.description: A set-like list of Rewards that have been added to the Order.

properties.name: rounding_adjustment
properties.description:
A positive rounding adjustment to the total of the order. This adjustment is commonly
used to apply cash rounding when the minimum unit of account is smaller than the lowest physical
denomination of the currency.

properties.name: service_charges
properties.description: A list of service charges applied to the order.

properties.name: source
properties.description: The origination details of the order.

properties.name: state
properties.description:
The current state of the order.
See [OrderState](#type-orderstate) for possible values

properties.name: taxes
properties.description:
The list of all taxes associated with the order.

Taxes can be scoped to either `ORDER` or `LINE_ITEM`. For taxes with `LINE_ITEM` scope, an
`OrderLineItemAppliedTax` must be added to each line item that the tax applies to. For taxes
with `ORDER` scope, the server generates an `OrderLineItemAppliedTax` for every line item.

On reads, each tax in the list includes the total amount of that tax applied to the order.

__IMPORTANT__: If `LINE_ITEM` scope is set on any taxes in this field, using the deprecated
`line_items.taxes` field results in an error. Use `line_items.applied_taxes`
instead.

properties.name: tenders
properties.description: The tenders that were used to pay for the order.

properties.name: ticket_name
properties.description:
A short-term identifier for the order (such as a customer first name,
table number, or auto-generated order number that resets daily).

properties.name: total_discount_money
properties.description: The total amount of discount money to collect for the order.

properties.name: total_money
properties.description: The total amount of money to collect for the order.

properties.name: total_service_charge_money
properties.description:
The total amount of money collected in service charges for the order.

Note: `total_service_charge_money` is the sum of `applied_money` fields for each individual
service charge. Therefore, `total_service_charge_money` only includes inclusive tax amounts,
not additive tax amounts.

properties.name: total_tax_money
properties.description: The total amount of tax money to collect for the order.

properties.name: total_tip_money
properties.description: The total amount of tip money to collect for the order.

properties.name: updated_at
properties.description: The timestamp for when the order was last updated, at server side, in RFC 3339 format (for example, "2016-09-04T23:59:33.123Z").

properties.name: version
properties.description:
The version number, which is incremented each time an update is committed to the order.
Orders not created through the API do not include a version number and
therefore cannot be updated.

[Read more about working with versions](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/orders-api/manage-orders/update-orders).

properties.name: checkout_options
properties.description:
Describes optional fields to add to the resulting checkout page.
For more information,
see [Optional Checkout Configurations](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/checkout-api/optional-checkout-configurations).

properties.name: description
properties.description:
A description of the payment link. You provide this optional description that is useful in your
application context. It is not used anywhere.

properties.name: idempotency_key
properties.description:
A unique string that identifies this `CreatePaymentLinkRequest` request.
If you do not provide a unique string (or provide an empty string as the value),
the endpoint treats each request as independent.

For more information, see [Idempotency](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/working-with-apis/idempotency).

properties.name: order
properties.description:
Describes the `Order` for which to create a checkout link.
For more information,
see [Square Order Checkout](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/checkout-api/square-order-checkout).

properties.name: payment_note
properties.description: A note for the payment. After processing the payment, Square adds this note to the resulting `Payment`.

properties.name: pre_populated_data
properties.description:
Describes fields to prepopulate in the resulting checkout page.
For more information, see [Prepopulate the shipping address](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/checkout-api/optional-checkout-configurations#prepopulate-the-shipping-address).

properties.name: quick_pay
properties.description:
Describes an ad hoc item and price for which to generate a quick pay checkout link.
For more information,
see [Quick Pay Checkout](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/checkout-api/quick-pay-checkout).

properties.name: accepted_payment_methods
properties.description: The methods allowed for buyers during checkout.

properties.name: allow_tipping
properties.description: Indicates whether the payment allows tipping.

properties.name: app_fee_money
properties.description:
The amount of money that the developer is taking as a fee for facilitating the payment on behalf of the seller.

The amount cannot be more than 90% of the total amount of the payment.

The amount must be specified in the smallest denomination of the applicable currency (for example, US dollar amounts are specified in cents). For more information, see [Working with Monetary Amounts](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/common-data-types/working-with-monetary-amounts).

The fee currency code must match the currency associated with the seller that is accepting the payment. The application must be from a developer account in the same country and using the same currency code as the seller. For more information about the application fee scenario, see [Take Payments and Collect Fees](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/payments-api/take-payments-and-collect-fees).

To set this field, `PAYMENTS_WRITE_ADDITIONAL_RECIPIENTS` OAuth permission is required. For more information, see [Permissions](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/payments-api/collect-fees/additional-considerations#permissions).

properties.name: ask_for_shipping_address
properties.description: Indicates whether to include the address fields in the payment form.

properties.name: custom_fields
properties.description: The custom fields requesting information from the buyer.

properties.name: enable_coupon
properties.description: Indicates whether to include the `Add coupon` section for the buyer to provide a Square marketing coupon in the payment form.

properties.name: enable_loyalty
properties.description: Indicates whether to include the `REWARDS` section for the buyer to opt in to loyalty, redeem rewards in the payment form, or both.

properties.name: merchant_support_email
properties.description: The email address that buyers can use to contact the seller.

properties.name: redirect_url
properties.description: The confirmation page URL to redirect the buyer to after Square processes the payment.

properties.name: shipping_fee
properties.description: The fee associated with shipping to be applied to the `Order` as a service charge.

properties.name: subscription_plan_id
properties.description:
The ID of the subscription plan for the buyer to pay and subscribe.
For more information, see [Subscription Plan Checkout](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/checkout-api/subscription-plan-checkout).

properties.name: closed_at
properties.description: The timestamp for when the order reached a terminal [state](entity:OrderState), in RFC 3339 format (for example "2016-09-04T23:59:33.123Z").

properties.name: created_at
properties.description: The timestamp for when the order was created, at server side, in RFC 3339 format (for example, "2016-09-04T23:59:33.123Z").

properties.name: customer_id
properties.description:
The ID of the [customer](entity:Customer) associated with the order.

You should specify a `customer_id` on the order (or the payment) to ensure that transactions
are reliably linked to customers. Omitting this field might result in the creation of new
[instant profiles](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/customers-api/what-it-does#instant-profiles).

properties.description:

Discounts can be scoped to either `ORDER` or `LINE_ITEM`. For discounts scoped to `LINE_ITEM`,
an `OrderLineItemAppliedDiscount` must be added to each line item that the discount applies to.
For discounts with `ORDER` scope, the server generates an `OrderLineItemAppliedDiscount`
for every line item.

__IMPORTANT__: If `LINE_ITEM` scope is set on any discounts in this field, using the deprecated
`line_items.discounts` field results in an error. Use `line_items.applied_discounts`
instead.

Details about order fulfillment.

properties.description: The order's unique ID.

properties.description: The line items included in the order.

properties.name: location_id
properties.description: The ID of the seller location that this order is associated with.

properties.description: The net money amounts (sale money - return money).

properties.description:

properties.description: The tenders that were used to pay for the order.

properties.description:

table number, or auto-generated order number that resets daily).

properties.description: The total amount of discount money to collect for the order.

properties.description: The total amount of money to collect for the order.

properties.name: total_tax_money
properties.description: The total amount of tax money to collect for the order.

properties.name: total_tip_money
properties.description: The total amount of tip money to collect for the order.

properties.name: updated_at
properties.description: The timestamp for when the order was last updated, at server side, in RFC 3339 format (for example, "2016-09-04T23:59:33.123Z").

properties.name: version
properties.description:
The version number, which is incremented each time an update is committed to the order.
Orders not created through the API do not include a version number and
therefore cannot be updated.

[Read more about working with versions](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/orders-api/manage-orders/update-orders).

properties.name: buyer_address
properties.description: The buyer address to prepopulate in the payment form.

properties.name: buyer_email
properties.description: The buyer email to prepopulate in the payment form.

properties.name: buyer_phone_number
properties.description: The buyer phone number to prepopulate in the payment form.

properties.name: location_id
properties.description: The ID of the business location the checkout is associated with.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The ad hoc item name. In the resulting `Order`, this name appears as the line item name.

properties.name: price_money
properties.description: The price of the item.

properties.name: accept_partial_authorization
properties.description:
If set to `true` and charging a Square Gift Card, a payment might be returned with
`amount_money` equal to less than what was requested. For example, a request for $20 when charging
a Square Gift Card with a balance of $5 results in an APPROVED payment of $5. You might choose
to prompt the buyer for an additional payment to cover the remainder or cancel the Gift Card
payment. This field cannot be `true` when `autocomplete = true`.

For more information, see
[Partial amount with Square Gift Cards](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/payments-api/take-payments#partial-payment-gift-card).

Default: false

properties.name: amount_money
properties.description:
The amount of money to accept for this payment, not including `tip_money`.

The amount must be specified in the smallest denomination of the applicable currency
(for example, US dollar amounts are specified in cents). For more information, see
[Working with Monetary Amounts](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/working-with-monetary-amounts).

The currency code must match the currency associated with the business
that is accepting the payment.

properties.name: app_fee_money
properties.description:
The amount of money that the developer is taking as a fee
for facilitating the payment on behalf of the seller.

The amount cannot be more than 90% of the total amount of the payment.

The amount must be specified in the smallest denomination of the applicable currency
(for example, US dollar amounts are specified in cents). For more information, see
[Working with Monetary Amounts](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/working-with-monetary-amounts).

The fee currency code must match the currency associated with the seller
that is accepting the payment. The application must be from a developer
account in the same country and using the same currency code as the seller.

For more information about the application fee scenario, see
[Take Payments and Collect Fees](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/payments-api/take-payments-and-collect-fees).

To set this field, `PAYMENTS_WRITE_ADDITIONAL_RECIPIENTS` OAuth permission is required.
For more information, see [Permissions](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/payments-api/take-payments-and-collect-fees#permissions).

properties.name: autocomplete
properties.description:
If set to `true`, this payment will be completed when possible. If
set to `false`, this payment is held in an approved state until either
explicitly completed (captured) or canceled (voided). For more information, see
[Delayed capture](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/payments-api/take-payments/card-payments#delayed-capture-of-a-card-payment).

Default: true

properties.name: billing_address
properties.description: The buyer's billing address.

properties.name: buyer_email_address
properties.description: The buyer's email address.

properties.name: buyer_phone_number
properties.description:
The buyer's phone number.
Must follow the following format:
1. A leading + symbol (followed by a country code)
2. The phone number can contain spaces and the special characters `(` , `)` , `-` , and `.`.
Alphabetical characters aren't allowed.
3. The phone number must contain between 9 and 16 digits.

properties.name: cash_details
properties.description: Additional details required when recording a cash payment (`source_id` is CASH).

properties.name: customer_details
properties.description: Details about the customer making the payment.

properties.name: customer_id
properties.description:
The [Customer](entity:Customer) ID of the customer associated with the payment.

This is required if the `source_id` refers to a card on file created using the Cards API.

properties.name: delay_action
properties.description:
The action to be applied to the payment when the `delay_duration` has elapsed. The action must be
CANCEL or COMPLETE. For more information, see 
[Time Threshold](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/payments-api/take-payments/card-payments/delayed-capture#time-threshold). 

Default: CANCEL

properties.name: delay_duration
properties.description:
The duration of time after the payment's creation when Square automatically 
either completes or cancels the payment depending on the `delay_action` field value. 
For more information, see 
[Time threshold](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/payments-api/take-payments/card-payments/delayed-capture#time-threshold). 

This parameter should be specified as a time duration, in RFC 3339 format.

Note: This feature is only supported for card payments. This parameter can only be set for a delayed
capture payment (`autocomplete=false`).

Default:

- Card-present payments: "PT36H" (36 hours) from the creation time.
- Card-not-present payments: "P7D" (7 days) from the creation time.

properties.name: external_details
properties.description: Additional details required when recording an external payment (`source_id` is EXTERNAL).

properties.name: idempotency_key
properties.description:
A unique string that identifies this `CreatePayment` request. Keys can be any valid string
but must be unique for every `CreatePayment` request.

Note: The number of allowed characters might be less than the stated maximum, if multi-byte
characters are used.

For more information, see [Idempotency](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/working-with-apis/idempotency).

properties.name: location_id
properties.description:
The location ID to associate with the payment. If not specified, the [main location](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/locations-api#about-the-main-location) is
used.

properties.name: note
properties.description: An optional note to be entered by the developer when creating a payment.

properties.name: offline_payment_details
properties.description:
An optional field for specifying the offline payment details. This is intended for
internal 1st-party callers only.

properties.name: order_id
properties.description: Associates a previously created order with this payment.

properties.name: reference_id
properties.description:
A user-defined ID to associate with the payment.

You can use this field to associate the payment to an entity in an external system 
(for example, you might specify an order ID that is generated by a third-party shopping cart).

properties.name: shipping_address
properties.description: The buyer's shipping address.

properties.name: source_id
properties.description:
The ID for the source of funds for this payment.
This could be a payment token generated by the Web Payments SDK for any of its
[supported methods](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/web-payments/overview#explore-payment-methods),
including cards, bank transfers, Afterpay or Cash App Pay. If recording a payment
that the seller received outside of Square, specify either "CASH" or "EXTERNAL".
For more information, see 
[Take Payments](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/payments-api/take-payments).

properties.name: statement_description_identifier
properties.description:
Optional additional payment information to include on the customer's card statement
as part of the statement description. This can be, for example, an invoice number, ticket number,
or short description that uniquely identifies the purchase.

Note that the `statement_description_identifier` might get truncated on the statement description
to fit the required information including the Square identifier (SQ *) and name of the
seller taking the payment.

properties.name: team_member_id
properties.description:
An optional [TeamMember](entity:TeamMember) ID to associate with 
this payment.

properties.name: tip_money
properties.description:
The amount designated as a tip, in addition to `amount_money`.

The amount must be specified in the smallest denomination of the applicable currency
(for example, US dollar amounts are specified in cents). For more information, see
[Working with Monetary Amounts](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/working-with-monetary-amounts).

The currency code must match the currency associated with the business
that is accepting the payment.

properties.name: verification_token
properties.description:
An identifying token generated by [payments.verifyBuyer()](https://developer.squareup.com/reference/sdks/web/payments/objects/Payments#Payments.verifyBuyer).
Verification tokens encapsulate customer device information and 3-D Secure
challenge results to indicate that Square has verified the buyer identity.

For more information, see [SCA Overview](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/sca-overview).

properties.name: amount
properties.description:
The amount of money, in the smallest denomination of the currency
indicated by `currency`. For example, when `currency` is `USD`, `amount` is
in cents. Monetary amounts can be positive or negative. See the specific
field description to determine the meaning of the sign in a particular case.

properties.name: currency
properties.description:
The type of currency, in __ISO 4217 format__. For example, the currency
code for US dollars is `USD`.

See [Currency](entity:Currency) for possible values.
See [Currency](#type-currency) for possible values

properties.name: address_line_1
properties.description:
The first line of the address.

Fields that start with `address_line` provide the address's most specific
details, like street number, street name, and building name. They do *not*
provide less specific details like city, state/province, or country (these
details are provided in other fields).

properties.name: address_line_2
properties.description: The second line of the address, if any.

properties.name: address_line_3
properties.description: The third line of the address, if any.

properties.name: administrative_district_level_1
properties.description:
A civil entity within the address's country. In the US, this
is the state. For a full list of field meanings by country, see [Working with Addresses](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/working-with-addresses).

properties.name: administrative_district_level_2
properties.description:
A civil entity within the address's `administrative_district_level_1`.
In the US, this is the county.

properties.name: administrative_district_level_3
properties.description:
A civil entity within the address's `administrative_district_level_2`,
if any.

properties.name: country
properties.description:
The address's country, in the two-letter format of ISO 3166. For example, `US` or `FR`.
See [Country](#type-country) for possible values

properties.name: first_name
properties.description: Optional first name when it's representing recipient.

properties.name: last_name
properties.description: Optional last name when it's representing recipient.

properties.name: locality
properties.description: The city or town of the address. For a full list of field meanings by country, see [Working with Addresses](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/working-with-addresses).

properties.name: postal_code
properties.description: The address's postal code. For a full list of field meanings by country, see [Working with Addresses](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/working-with-addresses).

properties.name: sublocality
properties.description: A civil region within the address's `locality`, if any.

properties.name: sublocality_2
properties.description: A civil region within the address's `sublocality`, if any.

properties.name: sublocality_3
properties.description: A civil region within the address's `sublocality_2`, if any.

properties.name: buyer_supplied_money
properties.description: The amount and currency of the money supplied by the buyer.

properties.name: change_back_money
properties.description:
The amount of change due back to the buyer. 
This read-only field is calculated
from the `amount_money` and `buyer_supplied_money` fields.

properties.name: customer_initiated
properties.description: Indicates whether the customer initiated the payment.

properties.name: seller_keyed_in
properties.description:
Indicates that the seller keyed in payment details on behalf of the customer.
This is used to flag a payment as Mail Order / Telephone Order (MOTO).

properties.name: source
properties.description:
A description of the external payment source. For example, 
"Food Delivery Service".

properties.name: source_fee_money
properties.description:
The fees paid to the source. The `amount_money` minus this field is 
the net amount seller receives.

properties.name: source_id
properties.description: An ID to associate the payment to its originating source.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
The type of external payment the seller received. It can be one of the following:
- CHECK - Paid using a physical check.
- BANK_TRANSFER - Paid using external bank transfer.
- OTHER\_GIFT\_CARD - Paid using a non-Square gift card.
- CRYPTO - Paid using a crypto currency.
- SQUARE_CASH - Paid using Square Cash App.
- SOCIAL - Paid using peer-to-peer payment applications.
- EXTERNAL - A third-party application gathered this payment outside of Square.
- EMONEY - Paid using an E-money provider.
- CARD - A credit or debit card that Square does not support.
- STORED_BALANCE - Use for house accounts, store credit, and so forth.
- FOOD_VOUCHER - Restaurant voucher provided by employers to employees to pay for meals
- OTHER - A type not listed here.

properties.name: client_created_at
properties.description: The client-side timestamp of when the offline payment was created, in RFC 3339 format.

properties.name: idempotency_key
properties.description: A unique string value to ensure the idempotency of the operation.

properties.name: shift
properties.description: The `Shift` to be created.

properties.name: breaks
properties.description: A list of all the paid or unpaid breaks that were taken during this shift.

properties.name: created_at
properties.description: A read-only timestamp in RFC 3339 format; presented in UTC.

properties.name: declared_cash_tip_money
properties.description: The tips declared by the team member for the shift.

properties.name: employee_id
properties.description: The ID of the employee this shift belongs to. DEPRECATED at version 2020-08-26. Use `team_member_id` instead.

properties.name: end_at
properties.description:
RFC 3339; shifted to the timezone + offset. Precision up to the minute is
respected; seconds are truncated.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The UUID for this object.

properties.name: location_id
properties.description:
The ID of the location this shift occurred at. The location should be based on
where the employee clocked in.

properties.name: start_at
properties.description:
RFC 3339; shifted to the location timezone + offset. Precision up to the
minute is respected; seconds are truncated.

properties.name: status
properties.description:
Describes the working state of the current `Shift`.
See [ShiftStatus](#type-shiftstatus) for possible values

properties.name: team_member_id
properties.description: The ID of the team member this shift belongs to. Replaced `employee_id` at version "2020-08-26".

properties.name: timezone
properties.description:
The read-only convenience value that is calculated from the location based
on the `location_id`. Format: the IANA timezone database identifier for the
location timezone.

properties.name: updated_at
properties.description: A read-only timestamp in RFC 3339 format; presented in UTC.

properties.name: version
properties.description:
Used for resolving concurrency issues. The request fails if the version
provided does not match the server version at the time of the request. If not provided,
Square executes a blind write; potentially overwriting data from another
write.

properties.name: wage
properties.description:
Job and pay related information. If the wage is not set on create, it defaults to a wage
of zero. If the title is not set on create, it defaults to the name of the role the employee
is assigned to, if any.

properties.name: canceled_date
properties.description:
The `YYYY-MM-DD`-formatted date when the newly created subscription is scheduled for cancellation. 

This date overrides the cancellation date set in the plan variation configuration.
If the cancellation date is earlier than the end date of a subscription cycle, the subscription stops
at the canceled date and the subscriber is sent a prorated invoice at the beginning of the canceled cycle. 

When the subscription plan of the newly created subscription has a fixed number of cycles and the `canceled_date`
occurs before the subscription plan expires, the specified `canceled_date` sets the date when the subscription 
stops through the end of the last cycle.

properties.name: card_id
properties.description:
The ID of the [subscriber's](entity:Customer) [card](entity:Card) to charge.
If it is not specified, the subscriber receives an invoice via email with a link to pay for their subscription.

properties.name: customer_id
properties.description: The ID of the [customer](entity:Customer) subscribing to the subscription plan variation.

properties.name: idempotency_key
properties.description:
A unique string that identifies this `CreateSubscription` request.
If you do not provide a unique string (or provide an empty string as the value),
the endpoint treats each request as independent.

For more information, see [Idempotency keys](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/common-api-patterns/idempotency).

properties.name: location_id
properties.description: The ID of the location the subscription is associated with.

properties.name: monthly_billing_anchor_date
properties.description: The day-of-the-month to change the billing date to.

properties.name: phases
properties.description: array of phases for this subscription

properties.name: plan_variation_id
properties.description: The ID of the [subscription plan variation](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/subscriptions-api/plans-and-variations#plan-variations) created using the Catalog API.

properties.name: price_override_money
properties.description:
A custom price which overrides the cost of a subscription plan variation with `STATIC` pricing.
This field does not affect itemized subscriptions with `RELATIVE` pricing. Instead, 
you should edit the Subscription's [order template](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/subscriptions-api/manage-subscriptions#phases-and-order-templates).

properties.name: source
properties.description: The origination details of the subscription.

properties.name: start_date
properties.description:
The `YYYY-MM-DD`-formatted date to start the subscription. 
If it is unspecified, the subscription starts immediately.

properties.name: tax_percentage
properties.description:
The tax to add when billing the subscription.
The percentage is expressed in decimal form, using a `'.'` as the decimal
separator and without a `'%'` sign. For example, a value of 7.5
corresponds to 7.5%.

properties.name: timezone
properties.description:
The timezone that is used in date calculations for the subscription. If unset, defaults to
the location timezone. If a timezone is not configured for the location, defaults to "America/New_York".
Format: the IANA Timezone Database identifier for the location timezone. For
a list of time zones, see [List of tz database time zones](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones).

properties.name: order_template_id
properties.description: id of order to be used in billing

properties.name: ordinal
properties.description: index of phase in total subscription plan

properties.name: plan_phase_uid
properties.description: the uid from the plan's phase in catalog

properties.name: uid
properties.description: id of subscription phase

properties.name: amount
properties.description:
The amount of money, in the smallest denomination of the currency
indicated by `currency`. For example, when `currency` is `USD`, `amount` is
in cents. Monetary amounts can be positive or negative. See the specific
field description to determine the meaning of the sign in a particular case.

properties.name: currency
properties.description:
The type of currency, in __ISO 4217 format__. For example, the currency
code for US dollars is `USD`.

See [Currency](entity:Currency) for possible values.
See [Currency](#type-currency) for possible values

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The name used to identify the place (physical or digital) that
a subscription originates. If unset, the name defaults to the name
of the application that created the subscription.

properties.name: idempotency_key
properties.description:
A unique string that identifies this `CreateTeamMember` request.
Keys can be any valid string, but must be unique for every request.
For more information, see [Idempotency](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/common-api-patterns/idempotency).

The minimum length is 1 and the maximum length is 45.

properties.name: team_member
properties.description:
**Required** The data used to create the `TeamMember` object. If you include `wage_setting`, you must provide
`job_id` for each job assignment. To get job IDs, call [ListJobs](api-endpoint:Team-ListJobs).

properties.name: assigned_locations
properties.description: Describes the team member's assigned locations.

properties.name: created_at
properties.description: The timestamp when the team member was created, in RFC 3339 format.

properties.name: email_address
properties.description:
The email address associated with the team member. After accepting the invitation
from Square, only the team member can change this value.

properties.name: family_name
properties.description: The family name (that is, the last name) associated with the team member.

properties.name: given_name
properties.description: The given name (that is, the first name) associated with the team member.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The unique ID for the team member.

properties.name: is_owner
properties.description: Whether the team member is the owner of the Square account.

properties.name: phone_number
properties.description:
The team member's phone number, in E.164 format. For example:
+14155552671 - the country code is 1 for US
+551155256325 - the country code is 55 for BR

properties.name: reference_id
properties.description: A second ID used to associate the team member with an entity in another system.

properties.name: status
properties.description:
Describes the status of the team member.
See [TeamMemberStatus](#type-teammemberstatus) for possible values

properties.name: updated_at
properties.description: The timestamp when the team member was last updated, in RFC 3339 format.

properties.name: wage_setting
properties.description: Information about the team member's overtime exemption status, job assignments, and compensation.

properties.name: action
properties.description: The Action to create.

properties.name: idempotency_key
properties.description:
A unique string that identifies this `CreateAction` request. Keys can be any valid string
but must be unique for every `CreateAction` request.

See [Idempotency keys](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/common-api-patterns/idempotency) for more
information.

properties.name: app_id
properties.description: The ID of the application that created the action.

properties.name: await_next_action
properties.description:
Indicates the action will be linked to another action and requires a waiting dialog to be
displayed instead of returning to the idle screen on completion of the action.

Only supported on SIGNATURE, CONFIRMATION, DATA_COLLECTION, and SELECT types.

properties.name: await_next_action_duration
properties.description:
The timeout duration of the waiting dialog as an RFC 3339 duration, after which the
waiting dialog will no longer be displayed and the Terminal will return to the idle screen.

Default: 5 minutes from when the waiting dialog is displayed

Maximum: 5 minutes

properties.name: cancel_reason
properties.description:
The reason why `TerminalAction` is canceled. Present if the status is `CANCELED`.
See [ActionCancelReason](#type-actioncancelreason) for possible values

properties.name: confirmation_options
properties.description: Describes configuration for the confirmation action. Requires `CONFIRMATION` type.

properties.name: created_at
properties.description: The time when the `TerminalAction` was created as an RFC 3339 timestamp.

properties.name: data_collection_options
properties.description: Describes configuration for the data collection action. Requires `DATA_COLLECTION` type.

properties.name: deadline_duration
properties.description:
The duration as an RFC 3339 duration, after which the action will be automatically canceled.
TerminalActions that are `PENDING` will be automatically `CANCELED` and have a cancellation reason
of `TIMED_OUT`

Default: 5 minutes from creation

Maximum: 5 minutes

properties.name: device_id
properties.description:
The unique Id of the device intended for this `TerminalAction`.
The Id can be retrieved from /v2/devices api.

properties.name: device_metadata
properties.description:
Details about the Terminal that received the action request (such as battery level,
operating system version, and network connection settings).

Only available for `PING` action type.

properties.name: id
properties.description: A unique ID for this `TerminalAction`.

properties.name: location_id
properties.description: The location id the action is attached to, if a link can be made.

properties.name: qr_code_options
properties.description: Describes configuration for the QR code action. Requires `QR_CODE` type.

properties.name: receipt_options
properties.description: Describes configuration for the receipt action. Requires `RECEIPT` type.

properties.name: save_card_options
properties.description: Describes configuration for the save-card action. Requires `SAVE_CARD` type.

properties.name: select_options
properties.description: Describes configuration for the select action. Requires `SELECT` type.

properties.name: signature_options
properties.description: Describes configuration for the signature capture action. Requires `SIGNATURE` type.

properties.name: status
properties.description:
The status of the `TerminalAction`.
Options: `PENDING`, `IN_PROGRESS`, `CANCEL_REQUESTED`, `CANCELED`, `COMPLETED`

properties.name: type
properties.description:
Represents the type of the action.
See [ActionType](#type-actiontype) for possible values

properties.name: updated_at
properties.description: The time when the `TerminalAction` was last updated as an RFC 3339 timestamp.

properties.name: checkout
properties.description: The checkout to create.

properties.name: idempotency_key
properties.description:
A unique string that identifies this `CreateCheckout` request. Keys can be any valid string but
must be unique for every `CreateCheckout` request.

See [Idempotency keys](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/common-api-patterns/idempotency) for more information.

properties.name: amount_money
properties.description: The amount of money (including the tax amount) that the Square Terminal device should try to collect.

properties.name: app_fee_money
properties.description:
The amount the developer is taking as a fee for facilitating the payment on behalf
of the seller.

The amount cannot be more than 90% of the total amount of the payment.

The amount must be specified in the smallest denomination of the applicable currency (for example, US dollar amounts are specified in cents). For more information, see [Working with Monetary Amounts](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/working-with-monetary-amounts).

The fee currency code must match the currency associated with the seller that is accepting the payment. The application must be from a developer account in the same country and using the same currency code as the seller.

For more information about the application fee scenario, see [Take Payments and Collect Fees](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/payments-api/take-payments-and-collect-fees).

To set this field, PAYMENTS_WRITE_ADDITIONAL_RECIPIENTS OAuth permission is required. For more information, see [Permissions](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/payments-api/take-payments-and-collect-fees#permissions).

properties.name: app_id
properties.description: The ID of the application that created the checkout.

properties.name: cancel_reason
properties.description:
The reason why `TerminalCheckout` is canceled. Present if the status is `CANCELED`.
See [ActionCancelReason](#type-actioncancelreason) for possible values

properties.name: created_at
properties.description: The time when the `TerminalCheckout` was created, as an RFC 3339 timestamp.

properties.name: customer_id
properties.description: An optional ID of the customer associated with the checkout.

properties.name: deadline_duration
properties.description:
An RFC 3339 duration, after which the checkout is automatically canceled.
A `TerminalCheckout` that is `PENDING` is automatically `CANCELED` and has a cancellation reason
of `TIMED_OUT`.

Default: 5 minutes from creation

Maximum: 5 minutes

properties.name: device_options
properties.description: Options to control the display and behavior of the Square Terminal device.

properties.name: id
properties.description: A unique ID for this `TerminalCheckout`.

properties.name: location_id
properties.description: The location of the device where the `TerminalCheckout` was directed.

properties.name: note
properties.description:
An optional note to associate with the checkout, as well as with any payments used to complete the checkout.
Note: maximum 500 characters

properties.name: order_id
properties.description: The reference to the Square order ID for the checkout request.

properties.name: payment_ids
properties.description: A list of IDs for payments created by this `TerminalCheckout`.

properties.name: payment_options
properties.description: Payment-specific options for the checkout request.

properties.name: payment_type
properties.description:
The type of payment the terminal should attempt to capture from. Defaults to `CARD_PRESENT`.
See [CheckoutOptionsPaymentType](#type-checkoutoptionspaymenttype) for possible values

properties.name: reference_id
properties.description:
An optional user-defined reference ID that can be used to associate
this `TerminalCheckout` to another entity in an external system. For example, an order
ID generated by a third-party shopping cart. The ID is also associated with any payments
used to complete the checkout.

properties.name: statement_description_identifier
properties.description:
Optional additional payment information to include on the customer's card statement as
part of the statement description. This can be, for example, an invoice number, ticket number,
or short description that uniquely identifies the purchase.

properties.name: status
properties.description:
The status of the `TerminalCheckout`.
Options: `PENDING`, `IN_PROGRESS`, `CANCEL_REQUESTED`, `CANCELED`, `COMPLETED`

properties.name: team_member_id
properties.description: An optional ID of the team member associated with creating the checkout.

properties.name: tip_money
properties.description:
The amount designated as a tip, in addition to `amount_money`. This may only be set for a
checkout that has tipping disabled (`tip_settings.allow_tipping` is `false`).

properties.name: updated_at
properties.description: The time when the `TerminalCheckout` was last updated, as an RFC 3339 timestamp.

properties.name: idempotency_key
properties.description:
A unique string that identifies this `CreateRefund` request. Keys can be any valid string but
must be unique for every `CreateRefund` request.

See [Idempotency keys](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/common-api-patterns/idempotency) for more information.

properties.name: refund
properties.description: The refund to create.

properties.name: amount_money
properties.description:
The amount of money, inclusive of `tax_money`, that the `TerminalRefund` should return.
This value is limited to the amount taken in the original payment minus any completed or
pending refunds.

properties.name: app_id
properties.description: The ID of the application that created the refund.

properties.name: cancel_reason
properties.description:
Present if the status is `CANCELED`.
See [ActionCancelReason](#type-actioncancelreason) for possible values

properties.name: created_at
properties.description: The time when the `TerminalRefund` was created, as an RFC 3339 timestamp.

properties.name: deadline_duration
properties.description:
The RFC 3339 duration, after which the refund is automatically canceled.
A `TerminalRefund` that is `PENDING` is automatically `CANCELED` and has a cancellation reason
of `TIMED_OUT`.

Default: 5 minutes from creation.

Maximum: 5 minutes

properties.name: device_id
properties.description:
The unique ID of the device intended for this `TerminalRefund`.
The Id can be retrieved from /v2/devices api.

properties.name: id
properties.description: A unique ID for this `TerminalRefund`.

properties.name: location_id
properties.description: The location of the device where the `TerminalRefund` was directed.

properties.name: order_id
properties.description: The reference to the Square order ID for the payment identified by the `payment_id`.

properties.name: payment_id
properties.description: The unique ID of the payment being refunded.

properties.name: reason
properties.description: A description of the reason for the refund.

properties.name: refund_id
properties.description: The reference to the payment refund created by completing this `TerminalRefund`.

properties.name: status
properties.description:
The status of the `TerminalRefund`.
Options: `PENDING`, `IN_PROGRESS`, `CANCEL_REQUESTED`, `CANCELED`, or `COMPLETED`.

properties.name: updated_at
properties.description: The time when the `TerminalRefund` was last updated, as an RFC 3339 timestamp.

properties.name: idempotency_key
properties.description:
A client-supplied, universally unique identifier (UUID) to make this [CreateVendor](api-endpoint:Vendors-CreateVendor) call idempotent.

See [Idempotency](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/common-api-patterns/idempotency) in the
[API Development 101](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/buildbasics) section for more
information.

properties.name: vendor
properties.description: The requested [Vendor](entity:Vendor) to be created.

properties.name: account_number
properties.description: The account number of the [Vendor](entity:Vendor).

properties.name: address
properties.description: The address of the [Vendor](entity:Vendor).

properties.name: contacts
properties.description: The contacts of the [Vendor](entity:Vendor).

properties.name: created_at
properties.description:
An RFC 3339-formatted timestamp that indicates when the
[Vendor](entity:Vendor) was created.

properties.name: id
properties.description:
A unique Square-generated ID for the [Vendor](entity:Vendor).
This field is required when attempting to update a [Vendor](entity:Vendor).

properties.name: name
properties.description:
The name of the [Vendor](entity:Vendor).
This field is required when attempting to create or update a [Vendor](entity:Vendor).

properties.name: note
properties.description: A note detailing information about the [Vendor](entity:Vendor).

properties.name: status
properties.description:
The status of the [Vendor](entity:Vendor).
See [Status](#type-status) for possible values

properties.name: updated_at
properties.description:
An RFC 3339-formatted timestamp that indicates when the
[Vendor](entity:Vendor) was last updated.

properties.name: version
properties.description: The version of the [Vendor](entity:Vendor).

properties.name: idempotency_key
properties.description: A unique string that identifies the [CreateWebhookSubscription](api-endpoint:WebhookSubscriptions-CreateWebhookSubscription) request.

properties.name: subscription
properties.description: The [Subscription](entity:WebhookSubscription) to create.

properties.name: api_version
properties.description:
The API version of the subscription.
This field is optional for `CreateWebhookSubscription`. 
The value defaults to the API version used by the application.

properties.name: created_at
properties.description: The timestamp of when the subscription was created, in RFC 3339 format. For example, "2016-09-04T23:59:33.123Z".

properties.name: enabled
properties.description: Indicates whether the subscription is enabled (`true`) or not (`false`).

properties.name: event_types
properties.description: The event types associated with this subscription.

properties.name: id
properties.description: A Square-generated unique ID for the subscription.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of this subscription.

properties.name: notification_url
properties.description: The URL to which webhooks are sent.

properties.name: signature_key
properties.description: The Square-generated signature key used to validate the origin of the webhook event.

properties.name: updated_at
properties.description:
The timestamp of when the subscription was last updated, in RFC 3339 format.
For example, "2016-09-04T23:59:33.123Z".

properties.name: key
properties.description: The key of the custom attribute definition to delete.

properties.name: booking_id
properties.description: The ID of the target [booking](entity:Booking).

properties.name: key
properties.description:
The key of the custom attribute to delete. This key must match the `key` of a custom
attribute definition in the Square seller account. If the requesting application is not the
definition owner, you must use the qualified key.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The UUID for the `BreakType` being deleted.

properties.name: object_id
properties.description:
The ID of the catalog object to be deleted. When an object is deleted, other
objects in the graph that depend on that object will be deleted as well (for example, deleting a
catalog item will delete its catalog item variations).

properties.name: key
properties.description: The key of the custom attribute definition to delete.

properties.name: customer_id
properties.description: The ID of the target [customer profile](entity:Customer).

properties.name: key
properties.description:
The key of the custom attribute to delete. This key must match the `key` of a custom
attribute definition in the Square seller account. If the requesting application is not the
definition owner, you must use the qualified key.

properties.name: group_id
properties.description: The ID of the customer group to delete.

properties.name: customer_id
properties.description: The ID of the customer to delete.

properties.name: version
properties.description:
The current version of the customer profile.

As a best practice, you should include this parameter to enable [optimistic concurrency](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/build-basics/common-api-patterns/optimistic-concurrency) control.  For more information, see [Delete a customer profile](https://developer.squareup.com/docs/customers-api/use-the-api/keep-records#delete-customer-profile).

properties.name: dispute_id
properties.description: The ID of the dispute from which you want to remove evidence.

properties.name: evidence_id
properties.description: The ID of the evidence you want to remove.

properties.name: invoice_id
properties.description: The ID of the [invoice](entity:Invoice) to delete the attachment from.

properties.name: attachment_id
properties.description: The ID of the [attachment](entity:InvoiceAttachment) to delete.

properties.name: invoice_id
properties.description: The ID of the invoice to delete.

properties.name: version

properties.name: types
properties.description:
An optional case-insensitive, comma-separated list of object types to retrieve.

The valid values are defined in the [CatalogObjectType](entity:CatalogObjectType) enum, for example,
`ITEM`, `ITEM_VARIATION`, `CATEGORY`, `DISCOUNT`, `TAX`,
`MODIFIER`, `MODIFIER_LIST`, `IMAGE`, etc.

If this is unspecified, the operation returns objects of all the top level types at the version
of the Square API used to make the request. Object types that are nested onto other object types
are not included in the defaults.

At the current API version the default object types are:
ITEM, CATEGORY, TAX, DISCOUNT, MODIFIER_LIST, 
PRICING_RULE, PRODUCT_SET, TIME_PERIOD, MEASUREMENT_UNIT,
SUBSCRIPTION_PLAN, ITEM_OPTION, CUSTOM_ATTRIBUTE_DEFINITION, QUICK_AMOUNT_SETTINGS.

current version of the catalog.
